title: Assets, Media Admission, Fingerprints, CSS, and Integrity · Failure Triage
summary: A preserved build trace contains one plausible source, path, encoding, metadata, parser, renderer, template, URL, asset, accessibility, security, cache, package, deployment, or release defect; locate the first failed boundary before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in bounded content-addressed asset manifest and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Admit assets by root, type, extension, signature where needed, size, dimensions, license, owner, and active-content policy.
Map source asset identity to collision-free public URL and output path without trusting basename or author traversal.
Hash canonical bytes with recorded algorithm and use the digest for cache-busting identity without embedding unstable metadata.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Asset Admission
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug publishing team handles case 5634 during an offline-first field documentation build. Build deterministic Python evidence to admit, fingerprint, rewrite, and reconcile bounded licensed assets; reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval; then replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: ssg-media-performance-l5hgck" that uses case 5489 to admit, fingerprint, rewrite, and reconcile bounded licensed assets. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Media Performance.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Media Performance into observable performance evidence. For Static-site publishing and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Media Performance, keep Media Performance connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-media-performance-l5hgck
# Competency: ssg-media-performance.
# Case 5489: an accessibility-led community newsroom release.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects.
# Changed case: replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG.
def evidence_ssg_media_performance_l5hgck(assets: int = 16, admitted: int = 14, rejected: int = 2, missing_rewrites: int = 0, digest_collisions: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_l5hgck = "l5hgck-kcgh5l-5489"
    assert evidence_variant_l5hgck
    evidence_axes_l5hgck = (
        "readerl5hgck",
        "source5489",
        "parsekcgh5l",
        "routel5hgck5489",
        "artifact5489l5hgck",
        "recoverykcgh5l5489",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_l5hgck)) == 6
    if min(assets, admitted, rejected) < 0:
        return False, assets, "asset-count"
    if admitted + rejected != assets:
        return False, admitted, "asset-reconciliation"
    if missing_rewrites > 0:
        return False, missing_rewrites, "asset-reference"
    if digest_collisions > 0:
        return False, digest_collisions, "asset-collision"
    assert admitted + rejected == assets
    return True, admitted, "fingerprinted-assets"
